query: I'm starting a new Python ML project from scratch. How do I set up reproducible dependencies so my CI matches what runs on my laptop and a teammate can reproduce my results six months from now?
expected_behavior: Recommends a lockfile-producing tool (uv lock, poetry lock, or pip-compile --generate-hashes) over bare 'pip install', and explicitly says to commit the resulting lockfile
Requires pinning the Python runtime version via .python-version file or requires-python == in pyproject.toml — not just a >= constraint
Recommends a strict-install command in CI (uv sync --frozen, poetry install --no-update, or pip install --require-hashes) that refuses to run if the lockfile is stale or missing
